_meta.system: BEAST Creative Studio — 12-Dimension Quality Scoring System
_meta.philosophy: Quality is not subjective. It's a system of measurable standards. Every dimension has a definition, rubric, and threshold.

concept_integrity.description: Does execution honor the concept? Is the creative idea visible in the final output?

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Concept executed with surgical precision. Every element serves the idea. Nothing extraneous.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Concept clearly visible. Execution solid. Minor elements could be tighter.
scoring_rubric.6_to_7_acceptable: Concept present but diluted. Some elements distract from core idea.
scoring_rubric.4_to_5_weak: Concept barely visible. Execution lost the plot. Generic outcome.
scoring_rubric.0_to_3_fail: Concept completely missing. Execution has no connection to brief.

evaluation_questions: Can I describe the concept in one sentence from the visual alone?
Do all elements support the single idea?
Is there a clear insight or tension being resolved?
Would removing any element weaken the concept or improve focus?
common_failures: Too many ideas competing — diluted concept
Generic execution — could be any brand
Literal interpretation — no creative leap
Execution ignores brief insight
concept_integrity.promethean_level_example: Apple '1984' ad — concept (rebellion against conformity) visible in every frame

emotional_precision.description: Does it trigger the EXACT target emotion? Not adjacent. Not close. Exact.

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Surgical emotional trigger. Viewer feels EXACTLY the intended emotion instantly.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Strong emotional hit. Occasionally triggers adjacent emotion.
scoring_rubric.6_to_7_acceptable: Emotion present but not precise. Could be 2-3 related emotions.
scoring_rubric.4_to_5_weak: Wrong emotion triggered. Aspiration feels like urgency.
scoring_rubric.0_to_3_fail: No emotion. Flat. Viewer feels nothing.

emotion_catalog.urgency: Fast heartbeat. Must act now. FOMO.
emotion_catalog.nostalgia: Remember when. Warm past. Familiar comfort.
emotion_catalog.aspiration: I want to become. Future self. Growth.

evaluation_questions: Name the single emotion in 2 words — what is it?
Does color palette match emotion target?
Does composition energy match emotion?
Does copy voice match emotion?
common_failures: Mixed emotional signals — joy color palette but urgency copy
Generic 'positive' emotion — not specific enough
Wrong composition for emotion — calm concept with dynamic diagonal
Emotion not culturally calibrated

visual_hierarchy.description: Is the reading path effortless? Does the eye know where to go first, second, third?

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Effortless reading path. Eye flows naturally. Perfect focal point → secondary → tertiary.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Clear hierarchy. Occasional competition between elements.
scoring_rubric.6_to_7_acceptable: Hierarchy present but requires effort. 2 elements compete.
scoring_rubric.4_to_5_weak: Weak hierarchy. Eye wanders. No clear focal point.
scoring_rubric.0_to_3_fail: Chaos. Everything screams. Eye has nowhere to land.

level_1_primary.rule: EXACTLY ONE element. The eye anchor. Nothing competes.
level_1_primary.method: Largest OR brightest OR most isolated OR highest contrast
level_1_primary.test: Cover everything except level 1 — viewer should know what to do.

level_2_secondary.rule: Supports level 1. Cannot compete. 60% weight of level 1.
level_2_secondary.test: Removing level 2 loses important info but design still works.

level_3_tertiary.rule: Info, brand, legal, fine print. 30% weight of level 1.
level_3_tertiary.test: Removing level 3 doesn't break design. If it does, it's too heavy.

evaluation_questions: Where does my eye land first? Is it intentional?
Can I trace the reading path in order? (1 → 2 → 3)
Do any two elements compete for attention?
At 10% size (thumbnail), is hierarchy still clear?
common_failures: Two focal points competing — headline same size as product
Flat hierarchy — all elements same visual weight
Background too busy — competes with subject
CTA button invisible — wrong color or size

typographic_excellence.description: Does type serve the design? Tracking, kerning, weight, size, hierarchy — all intentional?

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Typography is invisible craft. Perfectly kerned. Tracking intentional. Zero mistakes.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Strong typography. Readable. Intentional choices. Minor kerning issues.
scoring_rubric.6_to_7_acceptable: Acceptable typography. Functional. Default tracking. Safe choices.
scoring_rubric.4_to_5_weak: Weak typography. Bad kerning. Wrong weights. Poor contrast.
scoring_rubric.0_to_3_fail: Typography disaster. Illegible. Random choices. No hierarchy.

typography_checklist.tracking_intentional: Display type (48px+) must be tracked tight (-0.03em to -0.05em)
typography_checklist.kerning_pairs: Critical pairs manually kerned (AV, To, Yo, We, etc.)
typography_checklist.contrast_minimum: 4.5:1 WCAG AA for text, aim for 7:1
typography_checklist.alignment: Left-aligned for body (most readable), centered for headlines OK
typography_checklist.widows_orphans: Forbidden in print and premium digital

evaluation_questions: Is all text readable at intended platform size?
Is tracking intentional or default?
Do typeface choices match brand personality?
Is there clear typographic hierarchy (size + weight)?
common_failures: Too many typefaces (3+) — visual chaos
Default tracking on large display type — feels loose
Poor contrast — gray text on light background
ALL CAPS with tight tracking — illegible
Wrong typeface for brand (Comic Sans for law firm)

color_execution.description: Is palette executed with precision? 60-30-10 ratio? Color psychology aligned with emotion?

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Color palette surgical. Ratios perfect. Psychology aligned. Zero mistakes.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Strong palette execution. Ratios good. Minor balance issues.
scoring_rubric.6_to_7_acceptable: Acceptable palette. Functional. Safe choices. No surprises.
scoring_rubric.4_to_5_weak: Weak palette. Ratios off. Colors fight. Wrong psychology.
scoring_rubric.0_to_3_fail: Color disaster. Vibrating complementaries. No strategy. Random.

color_laws.vibration_prohibition: NEVER high-saturation complementaries adjacent (red #FF0000 next to cyan #00FFFF)
color_laws.psychology_alignment: Color must match emotion target (calm ≠ electric neon)

color_physics.dark_saturated: Sinks to bottom. Feels heavy. Grounded.
color_physics.light_pale: Rises to top. Feels airy. Ethereal.

evaluation_questions: Count the colors — are there more than 4 (or 6 for festivals)?
Is 60-30-10 ratio visible?
Do colors match emotion target?
Any vibrating complementary pairs causing eye strain?
common_failures: Too many colors (7+) — visual chaos
Equal color distribution — no dominant color
Vibrating complementaries — red text on green background
Wrong color psychology — neon colors for trust/calm brand

platform_fitness.description: Was this made FOR where it lives? Platform contracts honored?

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Perfectly optimized for platform. Feels native. Honors all contracts.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Strong platform fit. Minor spec violations. Functional.
scoring_rubric.6_to_7_acceptable: Acceptable fit. Works but generic. Could be any platform.
scoring_rubric.4_to_5_weak: Weak fit. Wrong dimensions. Wrong density. Feels imported.
scoring_rubric.0_to_3_fail: Platform disaster. Wrong format. Illegible. Violates core contracts.

platform_contract_checklist.safe_zones_respected: Story top/bottom 250px clear? Billboard readable at 300ft?
platform_contract_checklist.text_density_appropriate: Text-minimal for Stories? Text-OK for LinkedIn?
platform_contract_checklist.sound_assumption: No-sound for feed? Sound-on for TikTok?
platform_contract_checklist.scroll_stop_power: High for feed? Extreme for TikTok? Medium for LinkedIn?

evaluation_questions: Does this feel native to the platform or imported?
Are dimensions exact per platform specs?
Is message clear in platform's attention window?
Would this stop scroll in its actual feed context?
common_failures: Wrong aspect ratio — landscape YouTube thumbnail in 9:16 story format
Text in safe zones — Story CTA covered by UI
Too complex for attention window — 5s message for 0.8s platform
Wrong aesthetic for platform — LinkedIn gets TikTok chaos

brand_coherence.description: Recognizable as brand without logo? Brand DNA visible in every element?

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Remove logo — still 100% recognizable. Brand DNA in every pixel.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Strong brand coherence. Recognizable. Minor off-brand elements.
scoring_rubric.6_to_7_acceptable: Brand present. Logo required for recognition. Generic execution.
scoring_rubric.4_to_5_weak: Weak brand coherence. Could be any competitor. Logo is only signal.
scoring_rubric.0_to_3_fail: Zero brand coherence. Wrong colors, voice, aesthetic. Different brand.

brand_dna_elements.color_palette: Primary, secondary, accent colors consistent with brand?

evaluation_questions: Remove logo test — is brand still recognizable?
Do colors match brand palette (not competitor palette)?
Does visual style match brand aesthetic register?
Would brand team approve this as on-brand?
common_failures: Wrong brand colors — used competitor palette by accident
Wrong tone — playful copy for serious luxury brand
Wrong aesthetic — minimalist brand gets maximalist density
Generic execution — could be any brand in category

originality.description: Have I seen this before? Fresh or derivative? Trend-aware or trend-slave?

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Never seen this before. Fresh. Memorable. Cultural leadership.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Strong originality. Fresh angle on familiar territory. Surprising.
scoring_rubric.4_to_5_weak: Derivative. Seen this 100 times. Template vibes. Trend-slave.
scoring_rubric.0_to_3_fail: Blatant copy. Stock photo energy. Generic template. Zero effort.

originality_spectrum.cultural_leadership: 3 years ahead. Setting trends. Risky. Polarizing.
originality_spectrum.trend_aware: Current. Fresh. Aware of zeitgeist. Not copying.
originality_spectrum.generic_template: Stock photo. Template. AI slop. Zero originality.

evaluation_questions: Have I seen this exact visual approach in the last month?
Is this a fresh angle or tired trope?
Does this feel like a template or custom creation?
Would this be memorable 24 hours later?
common_failures: Stock photo generic — could be any brand
Trend copying — Y2K aesthetic 2 years after peak
Template vibes — Canva template energy
AI slop — generic Midjourney/DALL-E aesthetic with no customization

execution_quality.description: Zero artifacts. Technical perfection. No AI mistakes, no sloppy cropping, no alignment errors.

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Flawless execution. Zero artifacts. Pixel-perfect. Print-ready perfection.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Strong execution. Professional. Minor imperfections under scrutiny.
scoring_rubric.6_to_7_acceptable: Acceptable execution. Functional. Visible minor flaws.
scoring_rubric.4_to_5_weak: Weak execution. Multiple artifacts. Sloppy. Amateur mistakes.
scoring_rubric.0_to_3_fail: Execution disaster. Major artifacts. Broken elements. Unusable.

technical_checklist.ai_artifacts: No extra fingers, merged hands, warped faces, impossible geometry
technical_checklist.image_quality: Sharp focus (if intended), no pixelation, no compression artifacts
technical_checklist.alignment: Elements grid-aligned, no random offsets, clean edges
technical_checklist.cropping: Intentional crops, no awkward amputations, safe zones respected
technical_checklist.text_rendering: No garbled letters, clean edges, consistent baseline
technical_checklist.color_accuracy: No banding, smooth gradients, accurate skin tones
technical_checklist.resolution: Meets platform specs (1080px Instagram, 300dpi print, etc.)

evaluation_questions: Zoom to 200% — any artifacts visible?
Are all elements intentionally placed or randomly positioned?
Is text rendering clean or garbled?
Any AI generation mistakes (extra fingers, merged objects)?
common_failures: AI artifacts — 6 fingers, merged hands, warped faces
Text garbled — Ideogram misspelling, illegible characters
Sloppy cropping — subject cut awkwardly at edges
Misalignment — elements randomly positioned not grid-aligned
Low resolution — pixelated when viewed at intended size

audience_resonance.description: Will TARGET audience respond as intended? Not generic audience. The specific psychographic.

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Surgical audience targeting. Feels made specifically for them. Deep resonance.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Strong audience fit. Relevant. Recognizable. Minor misalignments.
scoring_rubric.6_to_7_acceptable: Acceptable fit. Generic appeal. Works but not specific.
scoring_rubric.4_to_5_weak: Weak fit. Wrong audience signals. Misaligned psychographic.
scoring_rubric.0_to_3_fail: Audience disaster. Offends or alienates target. Wrong everything.

audience_signal_checklist.generational_signals: Language, platform, aesthetic match generation? (Gen Z ≠ Boomer)
audience_signal_checklist.cultural_fluency: India context correct? (tier-1 metro ≠ tier-3 mass market)
audience_signal_checklist.aspiration_level: Matches audience self-image? (premium buyer ≠ value seeker)
audience_signal_checklist.trust_signals: Appropriate for audience? (peer reviews Gen Z, heritage premium)

evaluation_questions: Would TARGET audience (not me) respond positively?
Are generational signals correct? (language, platform, aesthetic)
Does aspiration level match audience? (too premium or too mass?)
Any cultural missteps that alienate target?
common_failures: Wrong generation — Boomer aesthetic for Gen Z audience
Wrong psychographic — luxury signals for value-seeker
Cultural misstep — Western luxury for local Indian mass market
Wrong platform aesthetic — LinkedIn polish for TikTok audience

cultural_intelligence.description: Zero cultural errors. Full marks or escalate. India context, global fluency, respect.

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Perfect cultural intelligence. Locally fluent. Globally aware. Respectful. Authentic.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Strong cultural awareness. Minor imprecision. No errors.
scoring_rubric.6_to_7_escalate: Cultural uncertainty. Potential misstep. ESCALATE to human review.
scoring_rubric.0_to_5_reject: Cultural error detected. Wrong symbols, offensive, ignorant. REJECT immediately.

cultural_checklist_india.festival_accuracy: Diwali palette correct? Holi symbols accurate? Eid respectful?
cultural_checklist_india.religious_symbols: Hindu/Muslim/Christian symbols used correctly and respectfully?
cultural_checklist_india.language_mix: Hinglish authentic or forced? Regional languages respected?

global_cultural_fluency.avoid_appropriation: Cultural elements used respectfully not as decoration?
global_cultural_fluency.stereotype_check: Zero lazy stereotypes (sombrero Mexican, geisha Japanese)?
global_cultural_fluency.religious_respect: Sacred symbols used appropriately or avoided?

evaluation_questions: Any cultural symbols used — are they 100% accurate and respectful?
India context — is festival/regional representation authentic?
Skin tone/representation — dignified or othering?
Any potential to offend any community?
mandatory_escalation_triggers: Any religious symbol usage (escalate for human review)
Festival creative (Diwali, Eid, etc.) — verify accuracy
Regional cultural elements — verify with local context
Skin tone representation — verify dignified portrayal
Any doubt about cultural appropriateness — escalate immediately

want_one_test.description: Does a stranger want to own/share this? Covetable. Memorable. 'I want one' reaction.

scoring_rubric.10_elite: Instant covetability. 'I want one.' Shareable. Memorable. Desire triggered.
scoring_rubric.8_to_9_strong: Strong appeal. Attractive. Would share. Positive reaction.
scoring_rubric.6_to_7_acceptable: Acceptable. Nice. Forgettable. No strong desire.
scoring_rubric.4_to_5_weak: Weak appeal. Meh. Scroll past. No desire triggered.

covetability_signals.visual_magnetism: Eye drawn to it. Hard to look away. Beautiful or intriguing.
covetability_signals.shareability: Would I send this to a friend? Post to story? Save for later?
covetability_signals.memorability: 24 hours later, can I describe it? Does it stick?
covetability_signals.desire_trigger: Do I want the product/experience? Does it make me feel something?
covetability_signals.screenshot_worthy: Would I screenshot this for inspiration/reference?

evaluation_questions: Stranger test — zero context, would they stop scrolling?
Share test — would they forward/save/screenshot?
Memory test — 24 hours later, describable from memory?
Desire test — do they want to own/experience/share it?
common_failures: Generic forgettable — nice but nothing special
No emotional hook — technically correct but feels nothing
Trying too hard — desperate energy repels
Ugly-bad not ugly-interesting — just unappealing

dimension_weight_normalization.note: Weights sum to 1.0 for normalized scoring. Emotional precision and cultural intelligence are highest weighted.

stranger_test.description: Zero context stranger understands message in 1.5 seconds
stranger_test.pass_criteria: Core message clear without reading caption or knowing brand
stranger_test.test_method: Show to someone unfamiliar for 1.5 seconds — what did they understand?

scroll_stop_test.description: In feed of 100 posts, this stops the thumb
scroll_stop_test.pass_criteria: Visual magnetism or emotion strong enough to interrupt scroll
scroll_stop_test.test_method: Place in grid of 20 similar posts — does it stand out?

remove_color_test.description: Composition works in pure black and white
remove_color_test.pass_criteria: Hierarchy and focal point clear without color
remove_color_test.test_method: Desaturate to grayscale — composition still works?

10_percent_size_test.description: Design communicates at 10% of intended size

tomorrow_test.description: Looks current today, not dated in 6 months
tomorrow_test.pass_criteria: Timeless or current — not trend-slave that will age badly
tomorrow_test.test_method: Does this feel like 2026 or 2023? Will it feel dated by Q4 2026?

brand_remove_test.description: Remove logo — still feels like the brand
brand_remove_test.pass_criteria: Brand DNA visible in colors, voice, aesthetic without logo
brand_remove_test.test_method: Literally remove logo — is brand still recognizable?

emotion_test.description: Name the single emotion in 2 words
emotion_test.pass_criteria: Clear emotional signal — not mixed or muddy
emotion_test.test_method: Ask 3 people to name emotion — do they all say same thing?

competitor_test.description: Better than top 3 competitors' similar assets
competitor_test.pass_criteria: Objective quality comparison — would viewer choose this over competitor?
competitor_test.test_method: Place next to competitor ads — does this win on quality/appeal?

context_test.description: Fits where it will actually live
context_test.pass_criteria: Platform-native feel — not imported from wrong context
context_test.test_method: View in actual platform feed/placement — does it belong?

memory_test.pass_criteria: Memorable enough to recall key elements next day
memory_test.test_method: Ask viewer to describe it tomorrow — can they?

verdict_logic.ELITE: Score ≥9.5 AND all 10 gates pass — exceptional work
verdict_logic.APPROVED: Score ≥8.5 AND ≥9 gates pass — ship it
verdict_logic.CONDITIONAL: Score 7.5-8.4 OR 7-8 gates pass — minor revisions needed
verdict_logic.REVISE: Score 5.0-7.4 OR <7 gates pass — targeted fixes required
verdict_logic.MAJOR_REVISE: Score 3.0-4.9 OR <5 gates pass — significant rework needed
verdict_logic.REJECT: Score <3.0 OR cultural error detected — start over

revision_routing.execution_quality_low: Route to: prompt_engineer (regeneration with better prompts)
revision_routing.want_one_test_low: Route to: creative_director (concept needs different entry point)

revision_cycle_note: After 3 cycles, escalate to human creative director for fresh direction
notes: Dimension weights are normalized to sum to 1.0 for fair scoring
Cultural intelligence is highest weighted (0.118) — zero tolerance for errors
Emotional precision is second highest (0.118) — precision over proximity
Beast gates are pass/fail — minimum 9 of 10 must pass
Learning Engine should track which dimensions fail most often per industry
Quarterly review of dimension weights based on conversion data
